formulas.name: Function Definition
formulas.definition: A block of code that takes arguments, performs operations, and returns values
formulas.notes: Used to group a set of statements together, making code more modular and reusable

formulas.name: Function Call
formulas.definition: The process of invoking a function, passing arguments, and receiving the return value
formulas.notes: Can be used to reuse a block of code multiple times in a program

formulas.name: Pass by Value
formulas.definition: A method of passing arguments to a function where a copy of the original value is passed
formulas.notes: Changes made to the argument within the function do not affect the original value

formulas.name: Pass by Reference
formulas.definition: A method of passing arguments to a function where the original variable is passed
formulas.notes: Changes made to the argument within the function affect the original value

formulas.name: Return Statement
formulas.definition: A statement that terminates the execution of a function and returns a value
formulas.notes: Used to provide the result of a function to the caller

formulas.name: Function Overloading
formulas.definition: A feature that allows multiple functions with the same name but different parameters
formulas.notes: Used to provide different implementations for the same function based on the input parameters

formulas.name: Function Overriding
formulas.definition: A feature that allows a subclass to provide a different implementation of a function already defined in its superclass
formulas.notes: Used to provide a more specific implementation of a function in a subclass
